remediates: codex §9 [P2]#1 — latency measured model reload, not steady-state embed (gate-affecting via cpu_feasible)
codex §9 [P2]#2 — ModelCfg.revision unset -> model_revisions recorded "default" (no reproducibility pin)
commits: 0942b5c6 test(0.8.3/Slice 15a fix-1): RED — latency-no-reload + pinned revisions
40b64f65 fix(0.8.3/Slice 15a fix-1): in-process model cache (steady-state latency) + resolved model-revision SHAs (codex §9 P2x2)

p2_1_fix.what: Added module-level _MODEL_CACHE keyed by (hf_id, revision, pooling); _load_model() loads once via _load_model_uncached() and reuses the (tokenizer, model) across all embed_texts calls. embed_texts no longer calls from_pretrained per call.
p2_1_fix.measure_latency: Loads once (cache) + one warmup forward EXCLUDED from timing, then times steady-state per-item forwards. batch_size=1 -> single-text ms_per_query; batch_size>1 -> batched per-item ms_per_doc (total batch time / batch len). run_probe now passes batch_size for ms_per_doc.
p2_1_fix.byte_identical: Verified under eu-0 CPU venv: cached == freshly-loaded == reload, byte-identical (np.array_equal) for bge-small; pinned-revision vectors == unpinned. Cache changes only caching, not numerics.

p2_2_fix.what: resolve_revision(hf_id) reads the local HF cache snapshot (models--<org>--<name>/refs/main, fallback snapshots/<sha>) honouring $HF_HUB_CACHE/$HUGGINGFACE_HUB_CACHE/$HF_HOME; torch/transformers-free + offline. build_model_revisions(names) produces the §9 model_revisions record. pinned_cfg(cfg) sets ModelCfg.revision to the resolved SHA and run_probe passes it to from_pretrained.
p2_2_fix.cache_key_unchanged: The .npy vector cache key is the model NAME (f'{cfg.name}.docs.npy'), NOT the revision; pinned_cfg preserves name via dataclasses.replace, so the currently-warming /tmp/fdb-s15a-cache-full vectors stay valid. Resolved SHA == current cache default.

tests.gate_unit: FATHOMDB_TESTS_NO_REBUILD=1 PYTHONPATH=src/python .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_s15a_embedder_probe.py -m 'not integration' -q => 9 passed, 4 deselected (was 6 passed pre-fix; +3 new: test_in_process_model_cache_loads_once, test_resolve_revision_from_local_cache, test_model_revisions_are_resolved_shas).
tests.red_confirmed: Before the GREEN impl, the 3 new unit tests FAILED (cache layer AttributeError; resolve_revision/build_model_revisions ImportError).
tests.integration_cpu: 2 new @pytest.mark.integration tests (test_embed_texts_steady_state_no_reload, test_model_cache_byte_identical_vectors) validated directly under dev/research/eu-0/.venv (gate venv has no torch; eu-0 venv has no pytest, so bodies exercised via eu-0 python): _load_model_uncached called once across 2 embed_texts + measure_latency; vectors byte-identical; all 5 model_revisions are 40-hex SHAs.

guardrails: No GPU/airlock; offline + $0; did NOT run --full; did NOT touch /tmp/fdb-s15a-cache-full/ or dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-s15a-embedder.json; did NOT modify decision_rule_083.py or the gold; no merge/push; no maturin develop; .npy cache key unchanged so the in-flight full run's warm vectors remain valid.
notes: The in-flight orchestrator --full process imported the OLD module, so it will still record model_revisions="default" and load-inclusive latency; this fix lands the pinned SHAs + steady-state latency for the NEXT (orchestrator) rerun. The resolved SHAs == the cache defaults, so the warm vectors stay valid.
resolve_revision falls back to "default" only if a model is absent from the local HF cache; under the eu-0 HF_HOME all 5 resolve to 40-hex SHAs (recorded above).
next_step_for_orchestrator: codex §9 re-review on the branch; on a future --full rerun (orchestrator-owned, eu-0 venv + HF_HOME) model_revisions will be the resolved SHAs and ms_per_query will be load-excluded steady-state.
